key_assumptions.source: IFRS timberlands at historical cost less depletion; book value per share from FY2025 annual report table; not fair-value NAV per acre

key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 Free Cash Flow $6.635M / weighted average shares = $0.37 per share (ADN.TO/investor-documents/ir-adn.to/Acadian-Annual-Report-2025-VF.pdf)
Maine internal harvesting improves margins; NB steady; carbon option out of Lawrence base

open_questions: Confirm fair-value per-acre marks vs IFRS book for owned NB/Maine timberlands.
Review Macer related-party governance and dividend policy if DRIP participation changes.
CEO transition: Malcolm Cockwell interim CEO per Q1 2026 letter; monitor permanent appointment.
Carbon credit issuance schedule not disclosed for base case.
thesis: Acadian Timber Corp. is a Canadian pure-play timberland owner and forest manager listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX: ADN). **[Fact]** The company manages approximately **2.4 million** acres across Eastern Canada and the Northeastern United States: **775,000** freehold acres in New Brunswick, **300,000** freehold acres in Maine, and timber services on roughly **1.3 million** acres of New Brunswick Crown licensed land. **[Fact]** Core operations are sustainable forest management and the sale of softwood and hardwood sawlogs, pulpwood, and biomass to about **85** regional customers; FY2025 timber sales and services revenue was **CAD 87.0 million** on **996,200** cubic metres harvested. **[Fact]** The company also earns fees from Crown land management, recreation access passes, waterfront leases, real estate reviews, and a voluntary carbon credit project on Maine conservation easement land (Anew – Katahdin Forestry Project). **[Fact]** Macer Forest Holdings Inc. owns approximately **52%** of outstanding common shares and reinvests **100%** of its dividends through the dividend reinvestment plan (DRIP).
why_market_wrong: The market may treat ADN as a simple high-yield timber stock whose **CAD 1.16** annual dividend is fully supported by recurring harvest cash flow, missing the trough-year owner-cash profile after lumpy **2024** carbon credit sales and the Maine operating transition. **[Fact]** FY2025 Free Cash Flow was **CAD 6.6 million** (**CAD 0.37** per share) while dividends declared were **CAD 20.9 million** (**CAD 1.16** per share); **2024** Free Cash Flow was **CAD 29.7 million** (**CAD 1.69** per share) aided by **752,100** voluntary carbon credits sold for **CAD 24.6 million**. **[Inference]** At **CAD 17.35** (June 10, 2026), the stock trades roughly **12%** below IFRS book value of **CAD 19.67** per share, a modest discount for a **~1.1 million** acre owned land base in a jurisdiction with long product life. **[Inference]** Predictive attribute: **timberland acreage REIT** plus **market structure discount** (small-cap Canadian income vehicle, related-party control, DRIP-dependent payout mechanics).
